query: bond types in glass ionomer cements

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Classification of GIC
retrieved_docs.content: GIC may be classified based on application as follows: Type I - Luting cement used for cementation of crowns and bridges Type II - Restorative cement used for aesthetic fillings Type III – GIC used as liners and bases Type IV – GIC used as pit and fissure sealants Type V – GIC used for orthodontic cementation Type VI – GIC is used for core build-up in highly mutilated teeth Type VII – Fluoride releasing light-cured GIC Type VIII – GIC for atraumatic restorative treatment (ART) Type IX – GIC used for pediatric and older adult restorations GIC may also be classified as follows:

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Mechanical properties such as wear resistance, strength, and fracture toughness are superior to conventional GIC but inferior to composite resins. Compomer bonds micro-mechanically with tooth structure but has no chemical bonding ability. Fluoride release is reduced compared to conventional GIC, but esthetics are superior.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement (GIC) is a self-adhesive restorative material. [1] Chemically, it combines fluoro-aluminosilicate glass powder and polyacrylic acid liquid. GIC has a broad spectrum of restorative adult and pediatric dentistry uses and exhibits potent anti-cariogenic action.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Restorative material: Thanks to its easy placement and better marginal adaptation, glass ionomer cement is popularly used in pediatric restorations. GIC is also indicated for restoring permanent teeth in low stress-bearing areas like class III and V lesions and is the material of choice in patients with a high risk of caries due to the release of fluoride. Luting agent: GIC can lute indirect restorations (metal and metal-ceramic), post, core, and orthodontic bands, and brackets. [4] [8] Pulp protection: GIC is used as a liner or base beneath metallic and composite restorations (sandwich technique). [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanism of Bonding
retrieved_docs.content: GIC chemically (ionic) bonds to the tooth structure (enamel and dentin). GIC bonds better to enamel than dentin because of the higher inorganic content in enamel. The mechanism of GIC adhesion to the tooth's inorganic structure involves a chelation reaction between the carboxyl groups of the polyacrylic acid and the calcium in the hydroxyapatite crystals of the tooth.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Along with amalgams and composites, GICs are used as core build-up materials. GICs are preferred as core build-up materials because of their property of chemical adhesion to the tooth structure. When used as fissure sealants and cervical restorations, GIC demonstrates good performance. [4] [5] [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Manipulation
retrieved_docs.content: The first increment is added to the liquid and mixed using a folding motion for 15 seconds within a limited area on the mixing pad (see Image . Folding Glass Ionomer Cement). The second increment is added to adjust the consistency. The resultant mixture should have a glossy surface (see Image . Freshly Mixed Glass Ionomer Cement). Such a surface indicates that an adequate number of carboxylic ions are available, crucial for forming chemical bonds with the tooth surface. Mixtures with an opaque surface should be discarded (see Image . Poor Mix Adhesion).

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties
retrieved_docs.content: The hybrid ionomer bonds chemically to the tooth structure. However, the ionic activity is reduced due to the presence of resin. This results in a lesser bonding tendency.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Wear resistance, fracture toughness, flexural, and diametral tensile strength of hybrid ionomer are superior to conventional GIC, whereas compressive strength is inferior. [2] [3] The translucency of the hybrid ionomer is superior to conventional GIC. Hybrid ionomer exhibits a slight tendency for shrinkage due to the polymerization of the resin component.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Reinforced Cement
retrieved_docs.content: The conventional GICs are suitable for restoring low stress-bearing areas like Class V and Class III cavities and sealing pits and fissures. However, they are not indicated in high-stress-bearing situations such as Class II cavities because of their low tensile strength (7 to 12 MPa). To widen its range of applications, attempts were made to improve the strength of GIC by the following methods: [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer cement has compressive strength equivalent to zinc phosphate cement, whereas its tensile strength is slightly higher than zinc phosphate cement. Type I GIC has lower compressive strength than Type II GIC. GIC is less stiff and has a lower modulus of elasticity comparable to dentin but lesser than zinc phosphate cement. [8] The resilience of GIC is similar to dentin, which is why it is known as man-made dentin.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Mechanism of Setting
retrieved_docs.content: The setting of GIC is through an acid-base reaction involving the following stages: Dissolution and decomposition: after mixing the powder and liquid, the acid dissolves the surface of the glass particles to release SiO 4 4- , Ca 2+ , Na + , and F - ions. Initial setting: the Ca 2+ released in the aqueous medium reacts with polyacrylic acid to form a 3-dimensional cross-linked structure. At this time, the material has low strength, and it can be carved using sharp instruments. Final setting: the setting reaction continues during the next 24 hours. During this time, SiO 4 4- forms the silica gel. The slow-moving Al 3+ ions enter the aqueous medium and cross-link the polyacrylic acid chains, displacing the Ca 2+ ions. This increases the final strength of the set cement. Maturation: the aluminum and calcium cross-linked polyacrylate chains get hydrated over time by absorbing water from the aqueous medium. This process is known as maturation. [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- The Role of Water in the Setting of GIC
retrieved_docs.content: GIC is water-based cement because water constitutes 11% to 24% of the set cement. Water plays a crucial role in the GIC setting. In the initial phases, it acts as a reaction medium. In later stages, it mediates the slow hydration of the cross-linked chains.

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Function -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Atraumatic restorative technique: Atraumatic restorative technique (ART) is a minimally invasive procedure that involves removing carious tissue with hand instruments without anesthesia. Restoration of the cavity requires an adhesive material like glass ionomer cement (GIC). ART is performed in those cases where routine dental treatment cannot be done because of a lack of facilities or accessibility to the dental clinic. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Polyacid Modified Glass Ionomer/Compomer
retrieved_docs.content: Compomer is formed by combining composite resin with glass ionomer. In this material, ion-leachable glass particles of GIC are added as fillers in the composite matrix. This combination integrates some of the composite resins and glass ionomer cement properties. Compomer is supplied as a single-component material. [27]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 1198
retrieved_docs.content: Nasogastric tube procedures classify the nasogastric tube, indicating that the range of clinical actions, techniques, and protocols are organized around the use, insertion, management, and care of the nasogastric tube.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 2387
retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery is a broad obstetric category that encompasses the specific outcome of فشل التوليد بالملقط (forceps delivery failure). Within this framework, الولادة بالملقط (forceps delivery) is directly linked to فشل التوليد بالملقط, indicating that the failure represents a particular adverse event or subcategory of the forceps delivery procedure. Consequently, both الولادة بالملقط and its associated failure, فشل التوليد بالملقط, are situated under the larger grouping of other procedures that induce or assist delivery, highlighting their interrelated roles in assisted vaginal birth and the importance of recognizing potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1895
retrieved_docs.content: Removal of cerclage from cervix is a medical intervention that is directly related to the cervical cerclage procedure (إجراء تطويق عنق الرحم). This step involves extracting the sutures that were placed around the cervix to reinforce cervical competence and prevent premature dilation, and it represents a specific phase within the overall management of cervical insufficiency. Recognizing the connection between the removal of the cerclage and the broader cerclage procedure is essential for appropriate patient care and timing of intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2927
retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery are classified as a subset of other procedures to assist delivery, indicating that these specific interventions are encompassed within the broader category of techniques used to facilitate and support the childbirth process.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 1691
retrieved_docs.content: Respiratory intubation and mechanical ventilation is recognized as both the specific medical procedure and the category that defines it, effectively classifying itself within the domain of airway management and ventilatory support.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2421
retrieved_docs.content: Antepartum Procedures is a medical category that includes, as its child, the subcategory Antepartum Procedures: Application, Insertion, Removal, indicating that the broader group of antepartum interventions is organized into specific actions of applying, inserting, and removing devices or treatments during the prenatal period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2106
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is categorized as a specific type of maternal condition that can complicate pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium, and it is considered a subcategory under the broader classification of other conditions or status of the mother complicating these reproductive processes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2407
retrieved_docs.content: In obstetric care, the broad category “other procedures to assist delivery” encompasses a more specific subgroup called “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery.” Within this subgroup, the Arabic‑named concept “فشل التوليد بالملقط” is identified as a child entity, indicating that it represents a particular type of procedure or complication related to assisted delivery. This hierarchical organization shows that “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery” is a child of “other procedures to assist delivery,” and “فشل التوليد بالملقط” is a child of “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery,” reflecting their interrelated roles in the classification of delivery‑assisting interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 1 -- Community 161
retrieved_docs.content: scarring into a unified classification scheme that supports recognition, documentation, and management of pregnancy‑related complications across multiple languages and clinical decision‑making pathways.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2912
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures is a broad category of obstetric interventions that encompasses specific techniques such as بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery, each classified as a child procedure within this group. Both بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery represent assisted vaginal delivery methods used to facilitate safe childbirth when spontaneous vaginal delivery is not optimal, highlighting the importance of tailored procedural options in maternal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 141
retrieved_docs.content: related and possibly synonymous with “other procedures to assist delivery,” reinforcing its placement within this overarching group. Finally, “Medical and surgical induction of labor” is a child of “Medical or surgical induction of labor” and is primarily mapped from “artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery,” illustrating that the combined medical‑surgical approach derives from the same membrane‑rupture technique. This integrated hierarchy clarifies how various cervical procedures, induction methods, and membrane‑manipulation techniques interrelate within the context of labor induction and assisted delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 209
retrieved_docs.content: constitute major complications of pregnancy, childbirth, and the puerperium. Recognizing the placement of each specific condition within its broader category supports comprehensive assessment, risk stratification, and coordinated management of pregnant patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2820
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم, a condition in which the uterine cervix is unable to maintain sufficient closure during pregnancy, leading to an increased risk of preterm birth or pregnancy loss. The cerclage technique involves placing a suturing material around the cervix to reinforce its structural integrity, thereby preventing premature cervical dilation. This intervention is employed as a therapeutic measure to manage cervical insufficiency and improve obstetric outcomes for affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 6 -- Community 2932
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Allowed qualifier” is recognized as an etiological descriptor that can be applied to several clinical entities. It may be used to indicate the cause of ولادة قبل الأوان (premature birth), تمزق (rupture or tear), and حَصاة (stone formation). Recognizing these etiological links helps clinicians accurately document and address the underlying causes of these conditions.
